title: Mobile EEG split-belt walking study

affiliations.name: J. Crayton Pruitt Family Department of Biomedical Engineering University of Florida Gainesville Florida USA
J. Crayton Pruitt Family Department of Biomedical Engineering University of Florida Gainesville Florida USA

description: This mobile brain body imaging (MoBI) study investigates electrocortical correlates of locomotor adaptation during split-belt treadmill walking. Thirty participants performed both abrupt and gradual split-belt walking paradigms with a 2:1 belt speed ratio while wearing mobile EEG, enabling examination of neural mechanisms underlying gait adaptation. Anatomical MRI data were also collected for each participant.
methods_description: Mobile EEG was recorded during split-belt treadmill walking tasks. Participants (n=30) completed both abrupt and gradual split-belt walking conditions with a 2:1 belt speed ratio. Anatomical MRI scans were acquired for structural reference.
